skill.name: defou-workflow
skill.description: Creates structured Simplified Chinese content reports from raw ideas using the Defou writing style.
skill.summary: Turn rough ideas into focused Defou-style content reports.
skill.author: wangdefou-dev

security_audit.summary: The weak cryptography alerts at EVALUATION.json lines 4, 54, 74, 104, 109, 114 and SKILL.md line 3 are false positives in prose and metadata. The shell execution alerts at SKILL.md lines 76, 77, and 84 are also false positives, but SKILL.md lines 74-78 contain a real instruction to write reports to a fixed absolute local directory.

medium_findings.title: Unscoped Local File Write Instruction
medium_findings.description: The workflow instructs the agent to save generated Markdown reports to /Users/Wangdefou/Claude Code Archive and create the directory if missing. This is legitimate for the author, but unsafe as a marketplace default because it can write outside a user's workspace without asking for a destination.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write instruction is explicit and includes a fixed absolute path. The behavior is not malicious, but it creates a clear local filesystem side effect.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography matches are prose or metadata, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language descriptions or prior audit text. They do not contain executable cryptographic logic or weak algorithm references.

low_findings.title: False Positive Shell Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution matches are a target directory, a filename example, and a Markdown code fence. No Ruby backtick execution or shell command invocation is present at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contents are plain Markdown instructions and formatting. There is no executable Ruby, shell syntax, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive Entropy Alert
low_findings.description: The high entropy alert appears to be caused by multilingual text and Markdown formatting. The file is readable documentation, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection showed normal YAML frontmatter followed by readable Markdown. No evidence found of encoded payloads or hidden binary content.

content.value_statement: Raw ideas often lack a clear angle and publishing structure. This skill turns notes into focused Simplified Chinese reports with hooks, scoring, and timing guidance.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes raw ideas and proposes three content angles.
Selects one angle that fits the Defou style.
Generates five structured content drafts from fixed templates.
Creates four opening hooks for each draft.
Scores each draft across four publishing potential dimensions.
Recommends a publishing time window for the content type.
limitations: Outputs must follow Simplified Chinese language rules.
The tone is fixed to the Defou style.
Publishing scores are heuristic, not audience analytics.
The default workflow includes a hard-coded local save path.

use_cases.title: Shape Rough Notes
use_cases.description: Turn scattered thoughts into clear content angles and ranked draft options.

use_cases.title: Plan Social Posts
use_cases.description: Generate hooks, bodies, scores, and timing suggestions for a Chinese social post.

use_cases.title: Review Content Direction
use_cases.description: Compare multiple angles before choosing the strongest message for a campaign.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze One Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Defou workflow to analyze this idea and create a structured report: [paste idea].

prompt_templates.title: Turn Notes Into Drafts
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these rough notes into five Defou-style content drafts with hooks and scores: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Content Angles
prompt_templates.prompt: Find three sharp angles for this topic, choose the best one, and explain the decision: [topic].

prompt_templates.title: Run Full Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the full Defou workflow on this source material, including angles, five drafts, hooks, scores, and timing: [paste material].

output_examples.input: A short note about why time management advice often fails.
output: The report identifies three possible angles.
It selects the strongest angle about structure over effort.
It creates five draft formats and hook options.
It scores each draft and recommends an evening posting window.

output_examples.input: A founder's notes about tool overload and productivity habits.
output: The report reframes the topic around decision quality.
It produces counterintuitive, list, case, growth, and experience drafts.
It highlights the best draft by total publishing score.

best_practices: Provide enough source context for sharper angle selection.
Review the selected angle before publishing the final draft.
Choose a safe output location before allowing file writes.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for topics that need factual verification only.
Do not treat the score as guaranteed performance data.
Do not allow automatic writes to an unwanted local path.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Markdown-style content report with angles, drafts, hooks, scores, and timing suggestions.

faq.question: What language does it use?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the assistant to output Simplified Chinese only.

faq.question: Can it publish posts directly?
faq.answer: No. It prepares content reports and does not publish to social platforms.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The workflow asks the assistant to write files to a fixed absolute local directory.

faq.question: Were the shell command alerts confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines are Markdown text, a path, and a filename example.

faq.question: Who is this best for?
faq.answer: It is best for creators and content teams writing opinion-driven Simplified Chinese posts.
